title: verbascum ovatifolium
intro: Verbascum ovatifolium, also known as the Large-leaved Mullein or Broad-leaved Mullein, is a perennial herb that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which contain mucilages that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Verbascum ovatifolium include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
